result.summary: Wrote the Phase 0-A COMPARISON PROTOCOL (Plan V4 item 0A-4) defining "unchanged" as three conjunctive conditions declared before measurement: RACE within a declared equivalence band on every one of N>=3 repeats, faithfulness verdicts exactly equal as a per-fixture-KEYED mapping (with an explicit rejection of set-based comparison, since sets discard fixture identity/ordering/multiplicity), and SHA-256 match on all deterministic artifacts. It fixes regression (RACE out of band OR any per-fixture verdict flip OR any deterministic-hash mismatch) vs noise (only RACE jitter within band), states the governing rule that RACE and faithfulness are never quarantined and an uncharacterizable band is a BLOCKING STOP rather than a proceed. The band value is left as a clearly-marked TODO to be filled after the N>=3 characterization run.

Built and committed the SECRET-SAFE Phase 0-A baseline manifest (Plan V4 item 0A-1) to /home/polaris/wt/phase0/docs/review_readiness/baseline_manifest.json on branch chore/review-readiness-phase0. Committed as 28ae7f7 (docs/review_readiness/ is tracked, not git-ignored — durable reviewer evidence).

WHAT WAS RECORDED:
- anchor_commit: b6e8ef5 (full SHA b6e8ef52aa29f5f2bfcbc87259125b5e30855073), subject "docs(phase0): box-3 GPU fix + search-diagnosis RESOLVED". Note that runtime code here == pre-initiative pipeline state; the first review-readiness runtime change is the NEXT commit 43214a2 (fix(audit_ir): lazy registry build) — verified via git log.
- requirements_lock_sha256: 14f798857739435f1fdcada49c5ffe1dc8faf95e8731959689ac39ca04d96d0e (via `git show b6e8ef5:requirements.lock | sha256sum`, 1064 lines, the lock AS OF the anchor).
- python_version: declared runtime 3.11.10 (from pyproject.toml target-version=py311 + baseline_test_state.md). Recorded a discrepancy note: the phase0 worktree shell interpreter observed at build time was CPython 3.10.12 (sandbox default python3); pipeline target remains 3.11.10.
- os_platform: uname -a = "Linux 88a2cef7eb24 6.8.0-101-generic ... x86_64 GNU/Linux".
- model_routing_config: code defaults from openrouter_client.py (4-role: Mirror z-ai/glm-5.2, Sentinel minimax/minimax-m2, Judge qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, Evaluator->Mirror) + outline_agent.py (outliner agent glm-5.2, outliner code deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro; generator default deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro). Plus live .env overrides recorded verbatim (all NON-SECRET slugs): OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL / PG_GENERATOR_MODEL / PG_MIRROR_MODEL / PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL / PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL / PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_MODEL = z-ai/glm-5.2; PG_FAITHLENS_MODEL=ssz1111/FaithLens; PG_NLI_MODEL=flan-t5-large. Note: .env overrides PG_GENERATOR_MODEL to glm-5.2 (differs from deepseek code default); Sentinel/Judge not overridden.
- secrets: read from /workspace/POLARIS/.env. 22 secret-named vars digested as {present, sha256(value)} ONLY — 19 present (CORE/EXA/FIRECRAWL/FIREWORKS/GEMINI/JINA/NCBI/OPENROUTER/OPEN_PAGERANK/SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR/SERPER/VAST/ZYTE API keys, OVH_APPLICATION_KEY/SECRET + CONSUMER_KEY, POLARIS_AUTH_SECRET, POLARIS_JWT_SECRET, VEXXHOST_PASSWORD), 3 absent/empty (DROPBOX_CLIENT_SECRET, GOOGLE_DRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET, ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET -> present:false). Explicitly excluded name-matched NON-secret config (all *_MAX_TOKENS, *_KEYWORD_MIN, PG_KEY_FINDINGS_ENFORCEMENT, PG_TOKEN_ACCOUNTING_*, POLARIS_AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRY=86400, POLARIS_CLOUD_TOKEN_PATH path, POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID public id) — documented in the manifest.

VERIFICATION: JSON validates. Ran an automated leak check grepping every raw .env value (all 22 secret values + all env values >=16 chars) against the manifest text -> SECRET_VALUE_LEAKS: NONE. CONFIRMED: no plaintext secret in manifest.

result.root_cause: Two separable facts. (A) The search MECHANISM is not statically broken: the THIN path checklist -> gap_ledger -> decide -> search_more_evidence is wired correctly. In outline_agent.py, _run_checklist (line 1354) names the gap from a verbatim quote of the question (the iter-2 P0-1 quote-grounding gate, lines 1418-1454; THIN question literally contains 'long-term cardiovascular safety'), records it via gap_ledger.add (line 1579), and _decide (prompt lines 1288-1304, rule 1) routes a PENDING gap to search_more_evidence. The committed acceptance_result_thin.json shows 1 search actually fired ('checklist[seed] named 1 gap(s): Safety::long-term cardiovascular safety' -> search_more_evidence[...] fetched 8), and my live run here reached a 2nd search fetch phase. The historical 'fires 0 searches' is an artifact of a LIVE, non-deterministic LLM+web run (Fable iter-1 verdict FABLE_GATE_iter1_VERDICT.md shows it once firing 3, once 0) plus fetch fragility, NOT a fixed code path. (B) The REAL, load-bearing defect for oracle-trustworthiness: the THIN positive control is ASSERTION-VACUOUS. run_thin() (acceptance_portable.py / acceptance_outline_agent.py lines 149-165) only REPORTS 'search_more_evidence_calls': search_calls and 'checklist_gap_events' as raw metrics in its return dict. There is NO assertion that search_calls >= 1, no pass/fail boolean, and no threshold check anywhere in run_thin, main(), or any wrapper (grep confirms only real_corpus_run.py / matched_comparison_run.py also merely report the same metric). Contrast the SATURATED negative control, which DOES compute an explicit gate: valid_negative_control = zero_searches and full_loop_ran (line 260). Consequence: the harness exits 0 and writes its JSON whether THIN fires N>0 OR 0 searches -- the positive control literally cannot fail, so it certifies nothing.
result.blocking_reason: PINNING BLOCKED on three independent grounds; the harness is NOT yet a trustworthy governing oracle. (1) ASSERTION-VACUOUS POSITIVE CONTROL (primary): run_thin() (lines 149-165) never asserts search_more_evidence_calls >= 1 and computes no pass/fail boolean, so a THIN run that fired 0 searches would still exit 0 -- the positive control cannot detect the very regression (dormant loop) it exists to catch. The negative control (SATURATED, line 260 valid_negative_control) is gated; the positive control is not. This asymmetry alone makes the oracle untrustworthy. FIX (in the copy, do not touch prod runtime): add a THIN pass gate asserting search_calls >= 1 AND outline_mutated, and have main() exit non-zero if it fails. (2) NON-DETERMINISTIC / NO FROZEN TRACE: the harness makes LIVE Serper + live web-fetch + live GLM-5.2 LLM calls with zero cassette/offline wiring (grep for cassette|replay|record|offline in the harness = no matches), even though a working deterministic oracle layer already exists beside it (tests/oracle/cassette.py + llm_cassette.py). A governing oracle for refactor-safety must replay a frozen trace and byte-compare a stable artifact; a live run's pass/fail swings with the web and model sampling (Fable iter-1 shows THIN firing 3 searches once and 0 another; SATURATED 3 once, 0 later). Until the THIN/SATURATED scenarios are recorded through llm_cassette (and a retrieval cassette) and pinned as a replayed golden artifact, 'pass' is not reproducible. (3) NOT RUNNABLE-TO-COMPLETION IN THIS ENV: the portable copy imports and resolves all paths cleanly (verified), but the live THIN run was KILLED at the 240s timeout (EXIT=124) still in the retrieval phase -- Playwright/Chromium cannot launch here (TargetClosedError on every browser fetch, archive.org/PyMuPDF fallbacks mostly 503). It needs a much larger wall budget and/or a working browser, or (better, and the real fix for #2) the cassette path so it needs neither network nor browser. RECOMMENDATION: before pinning, in acceptance_portable.py -- (a) add the THIN pass assertion (search_calls>=1 and outline_mutated) with a non-zero exit, (b) wire the two scenarios through tests/oracle/llm_cassette + a retrieval cassette, record once live, then pin the replayed run as the governing golden. The path portability + non-destructive-copy prerequisites (Plan V4 0A-2/0A-3) ARE satisfied; the oracle-trustworthiness prerequisite is not.

codex_points: Overall: 'No. The manifest passes 0A-1, but the comparison protocol is not yet acceptable under 0A-4.'

Manifest (0A-1): Secret-safe and correctly anchored to full commit b6e8ef52..., preceding the first runtime change (43214a2). Interpreter discrepancy is disclosed, but the ambiguous `python` command should eventually be pinned to 3.11.10 for reproducibility.

Protocol (0A-4): Correctly requires per-fixture-keyed exact faithfulness equality and forbids quarantining. BUT a TODO is not a declared equivalence band -- the protocol remains a DRAFT until a numeric/operational band is characterized and frozen.

Durability gap: The manifest has stated commit provenance (28ae7f7). Equivalent provenance was NOT supplied for the protocol; a tracked parent directory alone does not prove that file's current contents are committed. => commit the protocol.

Next action: Do NOT run N>=3 characterization. A positive control that cannot fail cannot characterize oracle sensitivity, and live external calls without a frozen replay confound oracle variance with changing inputs.

Required sequence before characterizing: (1) add an enforceable THIN success condition (assert search_calls>=1), (2) freeze/replay external responses (cassette), (3) demonstrate run-to-completion. THEN characterize, declare the band, and commit the finalized protocol.
Verbatim final two lines: 'P0A-ARTIFACTS-REVISE' / 'NEXT: FIX-HARNESS-FIRST'. Codex token usage: 15,813.

Verdict basis note: manifest 0A-1 is effectively PASS on secret-safety + anchoring; the REVISE verdict is driven by 0A-4 (band still TODO) + protocol durability (uncommitted) + the assertion-vacuous / non-deterministic harness.
